close_reason: Implemented in commit a0dbfe4 with package lint/test/typecheck passing.
description:
Comprehensive regression tests for the current-branch launch path.

## What to test

1. Current-branch launch does NOT call `prepareTicketWorktree()`.
2. tmux cwd is repo root / `checkoutPath`, NOT a worktree path.
3. Current-branch workers do NOT have `worktreePath` set.
4. Current-branch launch does NOT fail when checkout has dirty or untracked files.
5. Current-branch launch rejects detached HEAD by default.
6. Current-branch launch allows detached HEAD only when `workerExecution.allowDetachedHead === true`, and the worker record/evidence clearly captures the detached identity.
7. Handoff prompt contains all required instructions.
8. Handoff prompt discourages broad staging commands.
9. Handoff prompt does NOT mention worktrees.
10. `/bw run --workers N` can launch multiple current-branch workers simultaneously.
11. Worker runtime dirs are created in the expected location.
12. Worker record in registry has correct `executionMode`, `checkoutPath`, `branchName`, and `launchHead`.
13. Existing worktree launch behavior is preserved when mode is `worktree`.
14. `branchName` and `launchHead` are recorded BEFORE tmux launch.
15. Delegate/run launch feedback and launch notices use mode-appropriate language.
16. Current-branch launch errors reference `checkoutPath`/current branch, not `worktreePath`.
17. `landing.review.enabled` does not affect current-branch launch/review config, and worktree config is not consulted in current-branch launch.

## Tests location

`packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__/current-branch-launch.test.ts`

## Design rules

- Use mocked ProcessRunner and tmux backend for most unit tests.
- Include at least one integration-style test with a real git repo fixture.
- Test dirty-checkout tolerance explicitly by creating dirty and untracked files and proving launch succeeds.
- Test multiple concurrent launches without requiring extra serialization, IPC, locks, or reservations.
- Keep launch text assertions limited to Phase 2 surfaces; comprehensive dashboard/statusline UI remains in `sbdpi-qmd.5.3`.
- Include detailed assertion messages/log capture so failures explain which launch invariant regressed.

## Definition of Done

- All 17+ launch scenarios pass.
- Tests clearly document what they prove and why the no-lock/no-clean-gate invariants matter.
- Worktree-mode launch tests still pass.
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean.

comments.text: Implemented comprehensive Phase 2 current-branch launch regression coverage in packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__/unit/current-branch-launch.test.ts. Covered no worktree prep/clean gate/worktreePath, tmux checkoutPath, runtime dirs, registry metadata before tmux, handoff instructions/no worktree/broad staging guardrails, real dirty/untracked repo launch, detached HEAD reject/allow, /bw run --workers N multi-launch/no lock files, worktree-mode preservation, current-branch error wording, and landing.review vs workerExecution.review independence. Also fixed launch reviewStatus to use workerExecution.review for current-branch mode. Quality gates passed: npm run lint/test/typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension. Commit: a0dbfe4.

title: Add Phase 2 launch regression tests
